Incident Report: Tsukikawa Robotics K.K. — Operational/Cyber — 2026-05-17
Reference: IR-JP-2026-045, Prepared by Compliance Office (APPI/FIEA)
On 2026-05-15, a misconfigured access policy in our cloud DMS exposed an internal M&A workspace to a Singapore-based contractor of SkyLark Data Pte Ltd for 47 minutes; audit logs show list-view only and no file downloads.
The workspace contained draft board materials regarding a potential acquisition of Hoshizora Dynamics K.K. via a tender offer, which are undisclosed material facts under the FIEA until public announcement through our designated system at the Higashi Capital Exchange.
Per Compliance Memo CM-2026-11, we classified the materials as MNPI; however, the contractor’s NDA and DPA Schedule JP-3 prohibit use/disclosure, and no onward transfer occurred (vendor attestation 2026-05-16).
No “Kojin Bangō” (My Number) or resident records were stored in the affected workspace; the employee identifiers present were pseudonymous staff codes (e.g., HR-UID-7A3F), treated as individual codes under APPI and handled in line with data minimisation.
Cross-border transfer review: Data Export Approval DEA-2026-04 authorised HR analytics exports to SkyLark Data Pte Ltd (Singapore), but the M&A workspace was explicitly out of scope and remained blocked from scheduled exports.
Following internal notice, Mr. Kenji Sato-san withdrew consent for overseas processing of his onboarding form HR-ONB-12; we processed the withdrawal on 2026-05-16 and halted any further transfers of his record.
We received a DSAR from Ms. Aiko Nemoto on 2026-05-16 seeking access and erasure of her training log; disclosure was provided, and erasure completed in the backup retention cycle with a certificate of completion (Ticket DSAR-2026-088).
Retention adjustment: HR onboarding forms will be retained for three years instead of seven per Policy RET-JP-04; archived copies were trimmed to metadata-only to support legal hold without unnecessary personal data.
Security remediation: we completed key rotation, corrected the access policy, and enabled conditional access; the control is in place and there is no ongoing exposure.
We confirm that no transactions in issuer securities were executed by insiders or the contractor during the silent period; pre-clearance logs and trading restrictions were active and reconciled.
Notice obligations: although this did not meet the threshold for notification to the Shinsei Personal Data Commission, we documented the event and briefed the Kanto Securities Oversight Office due to potential FIEA sensitivity.
Any cross-border processing of personal data will continue only under informed consent or adequacy assessment in accordance with APPI, and My Number tokens remain localised and encrypted in a separate enclave.
For queries, contact Compliance at privacy@tsukikawa.example.jp; incident owner: Compliance Manager Haruto Miyazaki-san.
